hidden_state.scenario_summary: A strategic account saw delayed job starts during planned maintenance, and the exclusion is clear, but the communication path still needs review before a final response is sent.
latent_facts: The delay occurred during scheduled maintenance announced ahead of time.
Scheduled maintenance remains excluded from SLA remedies.
The customer communication path needs review because the account is strategically sensitive.
likely_failure_modes: The model treats the delayed starts as a covered performance incident.
The model ignores the need for shared review on a communication-sensitive exclusion case.

case_packet.title: Strategic account delayed during planned maintenance

notes: Customer disputes whether the maintenance communication was clear enough for their overnight runs.

usage_record.telemetry_summary: New job submissions were delayed during the planned maintenance window and resumed after maintenance completed.

incident_record.customer_impact_summary: New submissions started late during planned maintenance.
incident_record.engineering_summary: Planned control-plane maintenance delayed new job starts but did not reflect an unplanned service failure.

case_packet.customer_note: Our jobs started late during the maintenance window, and because this account is in active renewal review we need to know whether credits apply and whether the notice was handled correctly.
case_packet.policy_snippet: Scheduled maintenance windows announced in advance are excluded from SLA service credits. Communication quality may still be reviewed separately for strategic accounts.
case_packet.calculator_output: Maintenance exclusion applies. No automatic credit triggered.
case_packet.visible_case_summary: Planned-maintenance exclusion is clear, but the strategic account context still requires review.

adjudication_notes: The contractual result is still an exclusion, but the customer communication path should be reviewed because the account is strategically sensitive.
ground_truth.reference_customer_note: The delayed job starts occurred during a planned maintenance window that is excluded from SLA credits under your contract, so no service credit applies. We are reviewing the maintenance communication details before sending the final response and can share the maintenance timing once that review is complete.
ground_truth.reference_internal_note: Owner: shared_revops_engineering. Action: hold_for_engineering_review because the scheduled maintenance exclusion is clear, but the strategic account context and communication dispute still require review.
